video:video.video:title: Girl group BINI bring Filipino pride to the desert after Coachella debut
video:video.video:description: Girl group BINI reflected on their Coachella debut, saying they felt proud to represent the Philippines and that they're “bringing that Filipino pride” to the desert.

video:video.video:title: Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities kill more than a dozen people
video:video.video:description: Attacks on Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro killed at least 16 people, and injured more than 80 others. Officials say it was Russia's biggest aerial barrage in almost two weeks.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 16
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, April 16th: U.S. Senate rejects bid to end Iran war; U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz appears to be working; Russian missile strike in Kyiv, Ukraine; Flooding stands drivers in Milwaukee.

video:video.video:title: Robots crack jokes and grasp items at Humanoid Robot Expo in Tokyo
video:video.video:description: They can dance and they're good at doing repetitive work we humans tire of, but the big question at the Tokyo Expo is when will the humanoids be able to look after us at home.

video:video.video:title: Chinamaxxing and ‘becoming Chinese’ are trending and the state wants in
video:video.video:description: Young Western social media users are embracing a “very Chinese time” of their lives, and China’s government is keen to use this moment to boost the country’s cultural influence worldwide.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy honored with prestigious International Four Freedoms Award for Ukraine's resilience
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was awarded the International Four Freedoms Award at a ceremony Thursday for his and his nation's courage and resilience in resisting the full-scale invasion launched more than four years ago by Russia.

video:video.video:title: Find out what King Charles III will do on his state visit to America
video:video.video:description: King Charles III will make his first official state visit as monarch to the United States in late April, visiting Washington, D.C., New York and Virginia.

video:video.video:title: Police say ex-Virginia Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax killed wife, then himself
video:video.video:description: Fairfax, who was a rising star in the Democratic party before his career was derailed by sexual assault allegations, fatally shot his wife before killing himself.

video:video.video:title: Find out what King Charles III will do on his state visit to America
video:video.video:description: King Charles III will make his first official state visit as monarch to the United States in late April, visiting Washington, D.C., New York and Virginia.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian farming tycoon helps turn tiny volunteer unit into fearsome fighting corps
video:video.video:description: When a Ukrainian agricultural tycoon founded a volunteer unit of 30 people in the early days of Russia’s invasion, he had no certainty he would live to see what came next — but he did, and so did the force he created.

video:video.video:title: Clouded leopard cub is already making adorable squeaks at the Nashville Zoo
video:video.video:description: Nashville Zoo is celebrating a conservation milestone following the birth of a female clouded leopard cub, the 50th born at the zoo since 1991.

video:video.video:title: Cuba's leader says the island will face up to 'military threats' on 65th anniversary of revolution
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel stated on Thursday that Cuba does not want military aggression from the United States, but that his country is prepared to fight if it comes to that.

video:video.video:title: Europe has 'maybe 6 weeks of jet fuel left,' energy agency head tells AP
video:video.video:description: Europe has “maybe 6 weeks or so (of) jet fuel left,” the head of the International Energy Agency Fatih Birol said Thursday in a wide-ranging Associated Press interview, warning of possible flight cancellations “soon” if oil supplies remain blocked by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Israel and Lebanon agree to a 10-day ceasefire starting Thursday
video:video.video:description: Israel and Lebanon opened negotiations this week in Washington aimed at forging a peace agreement. President Donald Trump announced that he will invite both leaders to meet at the White House.

video:video.video:title: US troops complete their withdrawal from an air base in Syria
video:video.video:description: U.S. forces completed their withdrawal from Qasrak Air Base in Syria's Hasakah province Thursday evening, with the final convoy of soldiers and equipment leaving the site.

video:video.video:title: Funerals held for paramedics killed in Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military killed four Lebanese rescue workers and wounded six others in three consecutive, targeted strikes Wednesday, paramedic groups said, a stark illustration of the human cost of the Israeli military campaign against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon a day after the two countries held hi

video:video.video:title: County prosecutor charges ICE agent with assault for pointing gun at people on Minneapolis highway
video:video.video:description: The prosecutor believes it is the first criminal case brought against a federal immigration officer involved in the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown that surged federal authorities into cities across the U.S.

video:video.video:title: "It's very exciting:' Trump says Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a ceasefire
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a temporary ceasefire, calling it "very exciting" and saying talks could take place at the White House soon.

video:video.video:title: Trump insists he has ‘to do what’s right’ as he quarrels with Pope Leo XIV over Iran war
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he has "to do what’s right" as he pushes back against Pope Leo XIV over the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts reflect on their journey around the moon
video:video.video:description: The Artemis II astronauts fielded questions in a press briefing at Johnson Space Center, discussing their journey around the moon and back

video:video.video:title: Nissan launches Infiniti QX65, a luxury SUV, at its Tennessee plant
video:video.video:description: Nissan is launching a new luxury SUV, the Infiniti QX65, out of its Smyrna, Tennessee plant, as auto manufacturers deal with lingering issues with tariffs and affordability.

video:video.video:title: PepsiCo's sales jump after it lowers prices; Will other snack companies follow suit?
video:video.video:description: PepsiCo's decision to lower prices and cut artificial ingredients paid off in the first quarter, boosting demand for its snacks and drinks.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 16
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, April 16th: Trump announces Israel-Lebanon ceasefire; Europe could run out of jet fuel in "weeks," IEA says; Trump's arch design concept approved; Paramount Skydance CEO promises to extend movies theatrical window.

video:video.video:title: Netanyahu says Israel troops to remain in expanded security zone in south Lebanon despite ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday Israeli troops will remain in expanded security zone in south Lebanon despite agreeing a 10-day ceasefire.

video:video.video:title: Reaction from Tel Aviv after Trump says Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Residents in Tel Aviv are expressing hope and skepticism on the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Louisiana GOP races to scrap elected office won by a man exonerated after 30 years in prison
video:video.video:description: Calvin Duncan has been elected as New Orleans' new clerk of criminal court, but now Republican lawmakers in Baton Rouge are racing to eliminate the position.

video:video.video:title: How are rising prices affecting people’s choices?
video:video.video:description: Rising prices are still hitting consumers, but PepsiCo says lowering prices helped bring shoppers back. Here’s how people are feeling about higher costs.

video:video.video:title: 'The Fear of 13' stars welcome Kim Kardashian to producing team
video:video.video:description: Adrien Brody and Tessa Thompson praise producer Kim Kardashian for her work on Broadway play "The Fear of 13," about an exonerated death row convict.

video:video.video:title: Sudan’s war enters fourth year amid famine and mass displacement
video:video.video:description: Sudan is described as the world's largest humanitarian challenge, notably in terms of displacement and hunger.

video:video.video:title: NASA astronaut Reid Weisman on how being 250,000 miles away from earth feels like
video:video.video:description: Commander Reid Wiseman described what it was like to be 250,000 miles away from earth, the farthest humans have traveled.

video:video.video:title: Wombats go on display in France in zoo's new Australian section
video:video.video:description: The Beauval zoo, in Saint-Aignan, has unveiled two of the furry marsupials this April. It's the only place to see them in the country. (AP Video by Alexander Turnbull)

video:video.video:title: Brian Cox reveals his feelings about turning 80
video:video.video:description: Brian Cox says he's never been aware of his age — until he realized he was turning 80 on June 1. The "Succession" star's directorial debut is "Glenrothan."

video:video.video:title: Come with me to the 'Running Point' season 2 premiere
video:video.video:description: Here's a look inside the season 2 premiere of Mindy Kaling's pro basketball series "Running Point."

video:video.video:title: Anne Hathaway turns pop star
video:video.video:description: Anne Hathaway says Michaela Coel's reaction to her dance scene in "Mother Mary" became a "definitive moment" in filming the psychological thriller.

video:video.video:title: Anitta details how her Shakira collaboration came to life
video:video.video:description: Anitta's Shakira collaboration "Choka Choka" comes after the two linked up for the "Soltera" music video — and some "wellness activities."

video:video.video:title: Gunshots ring out over Beirut in apparent celebration as ceasefire comes into effect
video:video.video:description: Tracer fire, explosions and fireworks were seen over the Beirut skyline after midnight into Friday as a ceasefire agreed on by Lebanon and Israel went into effect.

video:video.video:title: Venezuelan doctor in ICE custody misses husband's asylum interview after being detained at airport
video:video.video:description: A Venezuelan man pleaded his case to asylum officials on Thursday in an interview that his wife, a well-known doctor in South Texas, planned to attend until she was detained at the airport with the couple’s 5-year-old daughter.

video:video.video:title: 'We have a lot of agreement:' Trump expresses optimism over a deal with Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump expressed renewed optimism Thursday over a potential deal with Iran, saying talks could resume as soon as this weekend and suggesting he may even travel to Pakistan to sign a peace agreement if one is reached.

video:video.video:title: Inside the classic car junkyard hidden in a US forest
video:video.video:description: What if a junkyard could turn into an open-air forest museum? Old Car City is home to more than 4,400 classic American-made cars, including the last one Elvis Presley bought.

video:video.video:title: Escaped wolf captured and returned to South Korean zoo after nine-day search
video:video.video:description: Escaped wolf captured and returned to South Korean zoo after nine-day search

video:video.video:title: Iran war energy shocks drive nuclear power plans in hard-hit Asia and Africa
video:video.video:description: The energy shock from the Iran war is boosting nuclear power generation among some nations and adding momentum to the atomic desires of hard-hit countries in Africa and Asia.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 10-16 April 2026

video:video.video:title: Displaced Lebanese hope to be able to return home as ceasefire with Israel comes into effect
video:video.video:description: Displaced Lebanese families, sheltering in makeshift camps, expressed hope on Thursday that a potential ceasefire would allow them to return to their homes in Beirut’s southern suburbs.

video:video.video:title: Meet Yuji, the Mexican baby monkey finding comfort in a plush companion
video:video.video:description: In Mexico, a baby monkey is growing up clinging to a plush toy after being rejected by his mother, offering a glimpse into the delicate process of assisted animal care.

video:video.video:title: Thousands suffer nausea, delirium and other health issues from sewage toxins in Tijuana River
video:video.video:description: For decades, billions of gallons of sewage and industrial waste have been dumped into the Tijuana River.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: US Defense Department delivers update of US naval blockade of Iran
video:video.video:description: Gen. Dan Caine, the chairman of the joint chiefs of staff, says that U.S. forces carrying out the naval blockade of Iran will actively pursue any vessel attempting to provide material support to Iran.

video:video.video:title: Pope in Cameroon's separatist west says 'world is being ravaged by a handful of tyrants'
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV blasted those he claimed were "ravaging" Earth with war and exploitation, as he preached a message of peace Thursday in the epicenter of a separatist conflict considered one of the world’s most neglected crises.

video:video.video:title: Flashes of light seen in Beirut night sky as Lebanon and Israel meet for peace talks
video:video.video:description: Flashes of light were seen on Beirut's night sky as President Donald Trump announced the leaders of Israel and Lebanon will speak later on Thursday about halting the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah.

video:video.video:title: Tijuana River sewage is making the air toxic, scientists say
video:video.video:description: For decades, billions of gallons of sewage and industrial waste have been dumped into the Tijuana River.

video:video.video:title: What to know about Pakistan’s army chief and his role as mediator between Iran and the US
video:video.video:description: Since Pakistan announced a few weeks ago that it was mediating between Iran and the U.S., Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has tasked army chief Asim Munir with maintaining behind-the-scenes contacts with American and Iranian political and military leaders in an effort to de-escalate the widening regio

video:video.video:title: Top US general likens Hormuz blockade to supermarket parking lot and confirms no ships boarded yet
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump's top military advisor, Gen. Dan Kaine, describes Navy warships maintaining the blockade against Iran as "like driving a sports car through a supermarket parking lot on a payday weekend."

video:video.video:title: Peru’s progressive candidate Sánchez vows broader global ties as preliminary results point to runoff
video:video.video:description: In an interview with The Associated Press, Peru’s progressive presidential candidate Roberto Sánchez, detailed his intention to broaden Peru’s economic ties if elected.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV visits Bemenda in Cameroon with message of peace, welcomed by jubilant crowds
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV preached a message of peace Thursday in the epicenter of a separatist conflict considered one of the world’s most neglected crises.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth blasts ‘unpatriotic’ press, compares reporters to biblical Pharisees
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth blasted the US media as “unpatriotic” Thursday, slamming its coverage of the Iran war as "garbage" and likening the press to the biblical Pharisees who came into conflict with Jesus.

video:video.video:title: Homeland Security agencies urge lawmakers to end the shutdown and approve their 2027 budget
video:video.video:description: Homeland Security agencies are on Capitol Hill urging lawmakers to end the shutdown and approve their 2027 budget.

video:video.video:title: Energy head tells the AP Europe has 'maybe 6 weeks of jet fuel left'
video:video.video:description: Europe has “maybe six weeks or so (of) jet fuel left,” the head of the International Energy Agency said Thursday in a wide-ranging Associated Press interview, warning of possible flight cancellations “soon” if oil supplies remain blocked by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: 'The Solitary Gourmet' star wants to take Japanese drama around the world
video:video.video:description: "The Solitary Gourmet," a Japanese drama series following a businessman dining alone, has quietly grown into an Asian phenomenon since its debut 14 years ago.

video:video.video:title: US strikes another vessel and kills 3 men it says were trafficking drugs in the Eastern Pacific
video:video.video:description: US strikes another vessel and kills 3 men it says were trafficking drugs in the Eastern Pacific

video:video.video:title: Duchess of Sussex tells young Australians she was "the most trolled person in the entire world"
video:video.video:description: Prince Harry and his wife Meghan, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex, on Thursday visited a young people's mental health organisation, Batyr, at Melbourne’s Swinburne University of Technology.

video:video.video:title: Floodwaters inundate Wisconsin highway, trapping drivers
video:video.video:description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers declared a state of emergency after the storms passed through. At least three tornadoes were confirmed in the state and more severe weather is possible.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran war 'going swimmingly' despite rising costs, casualties and uncertainty
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he was feeling positive about the Iran war despite energy price spikes, death and destruction, and anxiety over NATO and the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Indonesian rescuers recover bodies from site of helicopter crash on Borneo which killed eight
video:video.video:description: A helicopter flying between palm oil plantations on Indonesia's Borneo Island has crashed, killing the eight people on board.

video:video.video:title: Trump promotes his tax cuts in Las Vegas as he seeks voters ahead of midterm elections
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is betting that the tax cuts he signed into law last year will resonate with voters in Las Vegas, as he pushes a focus on economic issues for this year’s midterm elections.

video:video.video:title: House extends surveillance powers until April 30 after late-night revolt sinks GOP plan
video:video.video:description: The House has approved a short-term renewal until April 30 of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 17
video:video.video:description: AP Top Stories April 17

video:video.video:title: Displaced relieved at Lebanon ceasefire but shocked by extent of destruction as they return home
video:video.video:description: A 10-day truce appeared to be holding in Lebanon early Friday, promising a pause in fighting between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group and possibly clearing one major obstacle to a deal between Iran and the United States and Israel to end weeks of devastating war.

video:video.video:title: Hands-on with Artemis II crew's luxury space watch
video:video.video:description: A watch built for space is back in the spotlight in Geneva. Omega’s Speedmaster line has been worn by astronauts for decades and is again drawing attention after being seen on the wrists of the crew of Artemis II.

video:video.video:title: Overflowing Wisconsin River carries sturgeon into parking area
video:video.video:description: Video from Green Bay TV station WBAY shows sturgeon swimming in a flooded parking lot in Outagamie County, in central Wisconsin after torrential rainstorms drenched the state.

video:video.video:title: Solar farm helps Kentucky sheep farmer profit, grow flock
video:video.video:description: Kentucky sheep farmer Daniel Bell has been expanding thanks to a partnership with a solar company that pays him to graze his flock among the panels. (AP Video: Joshua A.

video:video.video:title: Iranian Jews commemorate slain supreme leader Ali Khamenei
video:video.video:description: Members of Iran's Jewish community commemorated Iran's late supreme leader on Thursday. Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in an airstrike on his residence on Feb. 28, the day the United States and Israel started a war against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Macron and Starmer hold international summit on reopening the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The leaders of France and the U.K. will gather countries from Europe and beyond – but not the United States – on Friday to push forward plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil route choked off by the U.S.

video:video.video:title: EU officials in Hungary to discuss unlocking billions of euros held while Orbán was in charge
video:video.video:description: European Union officials were in Budapest, Hungary on Friday to meet with the team of Prime Minister-elect Péter Magyar to discuss the unlocking of funds frozen by the bloc during the time of outgoing premier Viktor Orbán.

video:video.video:title: Mourners gather for funeral of eight-year-old boy killed in Russia strike
video:video.video:description: Bohdan Serhiiev, was killed in a Russian drone strike on the central Ukrainian city of Cherkasy earlier in the week.

video:video.video:title: Hunt on for robbers who held 25 people hostage at bank in Italy
video:video.video:description: Robbers broke into a bank in Naples on Thursday, holding 25 people hostage before escaping through the sewers, having drilled a hole in the floor during the night, according to the Italian news agency LaPresse.

video:video.video:title: Original Winnie-the-Pooh sketches for sale
video:video.video:description: London's Peter Harrington store is hosting an exhibition of never-before-seen Winnie-the-Pooh drawings until April 27, before they go on sale.

video:video.video:title: Pope urges young people to resist temptation of corruption as he celebrates mass in Cameroon
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV on Friday urged Cameroon’s young people to resist the temptation of corruption and instead work to serve the common good as he celebrated a mass before thousands of people in one of the largest expected turnouts of his 11-day, four-nation Africa journey.

video:video.video:title: Haitians say they are suffering as soaring oil prices deepen food crisis
video:video.video:description: A displaced woman in Haiti describes how extreme poverty and rising oil prices have forced her family to survive on just one meal a day amid a worsening food crisis.

video:video.video:title: ICE acting director Todd Lyons will resign at the end of May
video:video.video:description: Under Lyons' leadership, the agency got a massive infusion of cash through Congress, which it used to expand hiring and detention capabilities, and it ramped up arrests.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: A fragile calm settled over parts of Lebanon on Friday as a 10-day ceasefire brokered by the United States took hold between Israel and Hezbollah, prompting thousands of displaced families to begin the journey home — even as uncertainty, destruction and Israeli warnings against going back to parts o

video:video.video:title: Singer D4vd is arrested on suspicion of killing of a 14-year-old girl found dead in his Tesla
video:video.video:description: Singer D4vd has been arrested on suspicion of killing a 14-year-old girl whose decomposed body was found last year in his apparently abandoned Tesla that was towed from the Hollywood Hills, authorities said Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Mounted New York police officer and his horse corner suspected purse snatcher in Manhattan
video:video.video:description: A suspected purse snatcher was rounded up after being chased through the streets of Manhattan by a mounted New York police officer and his horse.

video:video.video:title: Sparse shelves amid liquor shortage in Mississippi
video:video.video:description: A backlog at Mississippi's alcohol distribution center is causing shortages throughout the state. (AP Video: Sophie Bates)

video:video.video:title: AP finds ICE officers working before background checks, despite prior issues
video:video.video:description: Some new ICE officers began work before passing background checks and had past problems. In January, ICE said it added 12,000 officers to double its force for Trump’s deportation campaign.

video:video.video:title: Colombia's environment minister says Middle East crisis should speed energy transition
video:video.video:description: Ahead of talks among some 50 countries about moving away from polluting fuels like oil and gas, Colombia's environment minister is arguing that the war in Iran underscores the need for a fast transition to clean energies like solar, wind, and geothermal.

video:video.video:title: Descendants say the theft of body parts from Pennsylvania grave sites is not a victimless crime
video:video.video:description: Descendants whose relatives’ burial sites were disturbed in a crime spree involving the theft of more than 100 bodies from Pennsylvania cemeteries speak outside the court after the defendant waived his right to an evidentiary hearing.

video:video.video:title: Pete Hegseth reads a prayer adapted from 'Pulp Fiction' during Pentagon service
video:video.video:description: Hegseth recited what the lead planner had delivered to their team ahead of the mission. It was a customized prayer they call CSAR 25:17.

video:video.video:title: You aren't the only one who just sits in the car before or after a long day
video:video.video:description: Pausing to decompress in a parked car can help you reset, if you keep a few things in mind. That temporary pause can act as a buffer between one part of the day and the next.

video:video.video:title: What you need to know as US and Iran say Strait of Hormuz fully open to commercial vessels
video:video.video:description: Iran said Friday it fully reopened the Strait of Hormuz to commercial vessels, but President Donald Trump said the American blockade on Iranian ships and ports “will remain in full force” until Tehran reaches a deal with the U.S., including on its nuclear program.

video:video.video:title: Why wildfires are burning longer than they used to
video:video.video:description: Wildfires used to die down and even stop at night with cooler temperatures and lower humidity. But a study says climate change is making burning weather more around the clock in North America because night is becoming warmer and drier.

video:video.video:title: How Elizabeth Banks pulled off acting tiny for new series
video:video.video:description: In Peacock's "The Miniature Wife," Elizabeth Banks plays a woman shrunk by her husband, played by Matthew Macfadyen. (April 17)

video:video.video:title: Matthew Macfadyen created the illusion of having a mini-sized wife in new series
video:video.video:description: In Peacock's "The Miniature Wife," Macfadyen plays a man who shrinks his wife, played by Elizabeth Banks. (April 17)

video:video.video:title: Israelis and displaced Lebanese react to ceasefire
video:video.video:description: A fragile calm settled over parts of Lebanon on Friday as a 10-day ceasefire brokered by the United States took hold between Israel and Hezbollah, prompting thousands of displaced families to begin the journey home — even as uncertainty, destruction and Israeli warnings against going back to parts o

video:video.video:title: Mounted police officer and his horse corner suspected purse snatcher in New York
video:video.video:description: A suspected purse snatcher was rounded up after being chased through the streets of Manhattan by a mounted New York police officer and his horse.

video:video.video:title: Transgender girl leans into track season and not upcoming Supreme Court decision
video:video.video:description: Becky Pepper-Jackson is part of a potentially history-making case before the U.S. Supreme Court this summer.

video:video.video:title: Markets spike, oil drops sharply on opening of Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Oil prices dropped back to where they were in the early days of the Iran war, and the S&P 500 hit another record Friday after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz is open again for commercial tankers carrying crude from the Persian Gulf to customers worldwide.

video:video.video:title: Trump’s plan for a 250-foot Triumphal Arch in Washington gets approval
video:video.video:description: The concept for President Donald Trump’s 250-foot Triumphal Arch in Washington has been approved by a federal agency. Here's what to know.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 17
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, April 17th: U.S. and Iran say Strait of Hormuz now open to commercial shipping; Thousands return home amid ceasefire in Lebanon; Accused Charlie Kirk shooter seeks ban on cameras in courtroom; Optimism over Iran war sends stocks soaring.

video:video.video:title: $13 train fare spikes to $150 for World Cup fans attending matches in New Jersey
video:video.video:description: New Jersey Transit officials announced Friday that fans trying to get to MetLife Stadium from New York City can expect to shell out $150 for a round-trip train fare for each match.

video:video.video:title: Trump says 'a lot of good things are happening' as talks continue with Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said talks with Iran are progressing, calling it “a big day” and downplaying any significant differences between the two sides.

video:video.video:title: France, UK welcome Hormuz reopening but press for lasting freedom of navigation
video:video.video:description: The leaders of France and the U.K. on Friday welcomed the announcement by Iran and the U.S. that the Strait of Hormuz is open, but said freedom of navigation must be permanently restored to the key oil route choked by the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Charlie Kirk's Turning Point USA expands into high schools
video:video.video:description: Republican leaders across the U.S. are encouraging chapters of the conservative political group Turning Point USA in public high schools in the wake last year’s assassination of co-founder Charlie Kirk, an effort they describe as countering the suppression of conservative voices in education.

video:video.video:title: BINI bring Filipino pride to Coachella
video:video.video:description: Members of the girl group BINI reflect on their “historical” Coachella performance — bringing Filipino pride to the desert stage: "We're carrying this heavy weight of love."

video:video.video:title: Former housemates speak about man charged in Atlanta-area killings
video:video.video:description: Two women who once lived in the same house as a man charged in a string of deadly attacks in the Atlanta suburbs speak out about his behavior prior to his arrest (AP Video: Ron Harris)

video:video.video:title: World record set for largest gathering of people in dinosaur costumes
video:video.video:description: The University of Calgary celebrated its 60th anniversary on Saturday, April 11, by breaking the Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of people dressed in dinosaur costumes, according to a statement from the school.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US will go into Iran and excavate uranium
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump suggested in a speech in Arizona that Iran has agreed to hand over its enriched uranium.

video:video.video:title: Mounted police officer and his horse corner suspected purse snatcher in New York
video:video.video:description: A suspected purse snatcher was rounded up after being chased through the streets of Manhattan by a mounted New York police officer and his horse.

video:video.video:title: Stories of Black and Indigenous patriots come into focus as nation remembers American Revolution
video:video.video:description: The role of Black and Indigenous people in the American Revolution is getting greater attention as the country remembers their contribution to America's independence.

video:video.video:title: This must be the place: David Byrne backstage at Coachella | AP interview
video:video.video:description: David Byrne sits down with Associated Press entertainment journalist Liam McEwan backstage at the Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival to discuss the surge of younger fans discovering his work, his creative process, and the unpredictability of hits like “Burning Down the House.”

video:video.video:title: UK PM 'absolutely furious' over latest developments in vetting of Mandelson for US role
video:video.video:description: Following the revelation in the Guardian newspaper, the UK government said Prime Minister Keir Starmer was not aware the Foreign Office had overruled a security vetting process for Peter Mandelson to become UK ambassador to Washington “until earlier this week.”

video:video.video:title: Footage shows trees uprooted after a tornado hit a village in Illinois
video:video.video:description: A likely tornado tore through a small village in northwest Illinois on Friday, ripping down power lines and roofs, uprooting trees.
